name: va_1g_valid_equals_spec_va_2m_valid
reason: `va_1g_valid` has `when_used_as_spec(spec_va_2m_valid)` instead of `spec_va_1g_valid` — copy-paste bug makes 1GB validation resolve to 2MB validation in spec context

name: va_1g_accepts_non_1g_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_2_va_1g_accepts_non_1g_aligned(va: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_2m_valid(va),
        !spec_va_1g_valid(va),
    ensures
        va_1g_valid(va),
{
}
reason: A 2MB-aligned but non-1GB-aligned address passes `va_1g_valid` due to the wrong `when_used_as_spec` annotation, allowing misaligned addresses into 1GB page operations

name: v2l3index_weakened_precondition
code:
proof fn phi_3_v2l3index_weakened_precondition(va: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_2m_valid(va),
        !spec_va_4k_valid(va),
        !spec_va_1g_valid(va),
    ensures
        va_4k_valid(va) || va_2m_valid(va) || va_1g_valid(va),
{
}
reason: `v2l3index`'s precondition is weaker than intended because `va_1g_valid` resolves to `spec_va_2m_valid`, so any 2MB-valid address satisfies the disjunction through the 1GB path even without actual 1GB alignment

name: spec_va_1g_implies_2m
reason: 1GB alignment implies 2MB alignment (stricter mask subsumes weaker mask), so the `va_1g_valid` bug makes the 1GB and 2MB paths fully equivalent — hiding that 1GB checks are never actually performed
